title: SDK Public Naming And Export Contract Implementation Tasks
principles: Prefer domain/runtime names over access-level names for reusable calculator schemas.
Reuse canonical schemas, branded ids, tagged errors and constructors from owning packages.
Preserve SDK compile-time safety and calculator-service runtime parity.
Keep the published SDK export map aligned with packed files.

globalVerification.commitPolicy: Commit after each coherent package-facing slice passes mandatory verification and parent review.
requiredBeforeFinalPR: packed SDK artifact import smoke test
evidenceRequired: Canonical naming migration summary and any transitional aliases
Changeset path and release-train impact, or explicit no-changeset rationale
Type-test evidence for SDK compile-time safety
Runtime parity evidence for calculator service, SDK and HTTP API

tasks.title: Rename reusable calculator execution schemas
tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list.

Before editing:

- Read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in `docs/architecture/*`.
- Identify the owning package for every schema, type, ID, service, layer and tagged error you touch.

Implementation rules:

- MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: `Data`, `Schema`, `Array`, `Chunk`, `HashSet`, `HashMap`, `Match`, `Context`, `Layer`, `Config`, `Service`, `Record`, `Result`, `Exit`, `Bun`, `Platform`, `Command` and `ManagedRuntime`.
- Do not fall back to plain TypeScript control flow, nullable values, mutable records, mutable arrays, `switch`, `Object.values`, `Object.entries`, ad hoc `_tag` objects, unsafe casts, stringly branching, custom env parsing or hand-rolled runtime wrappers when an Effect/schema/platform primitive fits.
- Reuse canonical schema-derived types, branded IDs, tagged errors, service contracts and constructors from the owning package.
- Do not create mirrored DTOs, local duplicate types, local duplicate schemas or transport-only shape copies when an owning schema or API/SDK contract already exists.
- Keep one-off Effect error handling and transformations inline at the callsite. Do not extract tiny mapper/wrapper helpers for single-use `Effect.mapError`, `Effect.catchTag`, `Effect.catchAll` or `Effect.catchAllDefect`.
- Browser/runtime code must consume browser-safe API/SDK exports instead of importing server-only internals.
- Package-facing changes MUST add or update a Changeset with `bun run changeset`, using a user-facing changelog summary. If the task is not package-facing, state that explicitly in the handoff.

Verification and handoff:

- Run this task's mandatory verification gates, including `bun run verification` unless the task explicitly documents a narrower gate.
- Report the Changeset path and release-train impact, or report why no Changeset was required.
- Report changed files, verification commands, outcomes and residual risks.
- Do not start or delegate another task. The parent agent must review, audit, verify and explicitly accept this task before the next task begins.

Task-specific scope: in `@whattax/calculators`, introduce canonical `CalculatorRun*` and `CalculatorServiceError` schema/type/data names for reusable calculator execution. Update package-local code and tests. Use transitional `PublicCalculation*` aliases only if needed for later tasks, and document them as transitional. Do not update SDK or HTTP API beyond what is required for this package to compile.
mandatoryVerification: rg audit proving reusable calculator execution canonical exports use CalculatorRun* names
completionCriteria: `CalculatorRunFacts`, `CalculatorRunRequest`, `CalculatorRunServiceRequest`, `CalculatorRunReport`, `CalculatorRunResponse`, `CalculatorRunResponseData` and `CalculatorServiceError` exist as canonical calculator-owned names.
Any retained `PublicCalculation*` aliases are explicitly transitional and not documented as preferred names.
No mirrored DTOs or duplicate schemas are introduced.
Parent agent reviewed canonical schema/type/id reuse.
Parent agent verified the Changeset.

tasks.title: Migrate SDK and HTTP API to canonical run names
tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list.

Before editing:

- Read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in `docs/architecture/*`.
- Identify the owning package for every schema, type, ID, service, layer and tagged error you touch.

Implementation rules:

- MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: `Data`, `Schema`, `Array`, `Chunk`, `HashSet`, `HashMap`, `Match`, `Context`, `Layer`, `Config`, `Service`, `Record`, `Result`, `Exit`, `Bun`, `Platform`, `Command` and `ManagedRuntime`.
- Do not fall back to plain TypeScript control flow, nullable values, mutable records, mutable arrays, `switch`, `Object.values`, `Object.entries`, ad hoc `_tag` objects, unsafe casts, stringly branching, custom env parsing or hand-rolled runtime wrappers when an Effect/schema/platform primitive fits.
- Reuse canonical schema-derived types, branded IDs, tagged errors, service contracts and constructors from the owning package.
- Do not create mirrored DTOs, local duplicate types, local duplicate schemas or transport-only shape copies when an owning schema or API/SDK contract already exists.
- Keep one-off Effect error handling and transformations inline at the callsite. Do not extract tiny mapper/wrapper helpers for single-use `Effect.mapError`, `Effect.catchTag`, `Effect.catchAll` or `Effect.catchAllDefect`.
- Browser/runtime code must consume browser-safe API/SDK exports instead of importing server-only internals.
- Package-facing changes MUST add or update a Changeset with `bun run changeset`, using a user-facing changelog summary. If the task is not package-facing, state that explicitly in the handoff.

Verification and handoff:

- Run this task's mandatory verification gates, including `bun run verification` unless the task explicitly documents a narrower gate.
- Report the Changeset path and release-train impact, or report why no Changeset was required.
- Report changed files, verification commands, outcomes and residual risks.
- Do not start or delegate another task. The parent agent must review, audit, verify and explicitly accept this task before the next task begins.

Task-specific scope: update `@whattax/sdk` and `@whattax/http-api` imports/types/docs/tests to consume `CalculatorRun*` and `CalculatorServiceError` names. Keep HTTP route-only names such as `PublicErrorEnvelope` in HTTP API. Preserve the SDK request-preserving Effect facade and plain facade behavior.
mandatoryVerification: rg audit proving SDK preferred imports use CalculatorRun* names
completionCriteria: SDK descriptor generics use `CalculatorRunFacts` and `CalculatorRunReport` names.
SDK error details use `CalculatorServiceError` for canonical calculator failures.
HTTP calculate route still uses canonical calculator run request/response schemas while preserving HTTP envelopes.
Runtime parity tests still compare SDK, calculator service and HTTP API success/error behavior.
Type tests still prove mismatched facts and unsupported module capabilities fail at compile time.
Parent agent verified no SDK dependency on `@whattax/http-api`.

tasks.title: Lock published SDK export map and schema exports
tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list.

- Do not start or delegate another task. The parent agent must review, audit, verify and explicitly accept this task before the next task begins.

Task-specific scope: update the SDK export map to match packed files, remove packed-missing `source` export conditions unless source files are intentionally included, and make `@whattax/sdk/schemas` re-export canonical `CalculatorRun*`, `CalculatorServiceError` and SDK-owned error schemas. Add export-map and packed-artifact smoke tests or scripts.
mandatoryVerification: npm pack --dry-run --json packages/sdk/typescript or equivalent packed file audit
packed artifact import smoke test for root, AU, Effect, AU Effect, schemas and testing entrypoints
completionCriteria: Published SDK export paths resolve only to files present in the packed artifact.
`@whattax/sdk/schemas` re-exports canonical calculator-run schemas from `@whattax/calculators` without duplication.
Root, AU and schemas entrypoints remain browser-safe.
Packed artifact no longer includes compiled test files, source files or source-only export conditions unless intentionally documented.
Parent agent verified packed artifact evidence and Changeset impact.

tasks.implementationPrompt:

Task-specific scope: update public docs, package READMEs and downstream validation to use the final `CalculatorRun*`/`CalculatorServiceError` names and locked SDK export contract. Keep public WhatTax docs neutral and do not name private downstream product details.
outputs: Downstream workspace validation evidence recorded in handoff or active exec plan
mandatoryVerification: bun run changeset status --verbose in WhatTax
downstream browser/build check if SDK is consumed by browser code
downstream type-level misuse assertion remains valid
rg audit proving public docs advertise CalculatorRun* names and not PublicCalculation* names as preferred SDK/calculator vocabulary
bun run changeset or explicit no-changeset rationale
completionCriteria: Architecture docs explain `CalculatorRun*` ownership and keep HTTP-only public names in HTTP API.
SDK README and package docs advertise the locked export contract.
Downstream workspace consumes the SDK successfully with the final exported names.
No public WhatTax docs mention private downstream product names.
Parent agent reviewed downstream validation evidence and accepted the no-changeset or Changeset decision.
